channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas says it will only release American-Israeli hostage if ceasefire deal is implemented
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas said Saturday it will only release an American-Israeli and the bodies of four other hostages if Israel implements their ceasefire agreement, calling it an “exceptional deal” aimed at getting the truce back on track.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: The Alien Enemies Act: What to know about a 1798 law that Trump has invoked for deportations
item.description: President Donald Trump has invoked the Alien Enemies Act for the first time since World War II, granting himself sweeping powers under a centuries-old law to deport people associated with a Venezuelan gang.
item.dc:creator: Tim Sullivan And Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US launches ‘decisive and powerful’ strikes against Houthis in Yemen
item.description: President Trump said the United States has launched “decisive and powerful” strikes against Houthi militants in Yemen, warning that the military “will use overwhelming lethal force” until the Iran-backed rebels stop their attacks on cargo ships and free up shipping lanes in the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump invokes 18th century law to speed deportations, judge stalls it hours later
item.description: A federal judge barred the Trump administration Saturday from carrying out deportations under a sweeping 18th century law that the president invoked just hours earlier to speed removal of Venezuelan gang members from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Freed Israeli hostage speaks for the first time about his 505 days of surviving Hamas hell
item.description: Freed hostage Tal Shoham shares his 505-day ordeal under Hamas, surviving abuse and psychological warfare while clinging to hope. His story is one of resilience, defiance and the will to return home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VOA journalists put on administrative leave after Trump axes parent agency
item.description: Journalists at Voice of America (VOA) were put on administrative leave on Saturday, a day after President Trump signed an executive order aimed at eliminating the U.S. Agency for Global Media (USAGM), VOA’s parent company.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Starmer challenges Putin to prove he is serious about peace by signing up to Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Saturday challenged Russian President Vladimir Putin to sign up to a ceasefire in its war against Ukraine if he is serious about peace, and said allies will keep increasing the pressure on the Kremlin, including by moving planning for a peacekeepi
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's pick for hostage negotiations envoy withdraws nomination but will continue on hostage issues
item.description: Adam Boehler, President Donald Trump’s nominee to become special envoy for hostage affairs, has withdrawn his nomination to avoid a requirement that he divest from his investment business.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas says American-Israeli hostage will only be freed if ceasefire is implemented
item.description: Hamas said Saturday that it will only release Israeli-American hostage Edan Alexander if Israel implements the existing ceasefire agreement in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says ISIS chief killed in Iraq
item.description: President Trump on Friday announced that ISIS’s second in command was killed in Iraq this week, touting it as an example of his administration’s “peace through strength” military philosophy.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Over 100,000 people join protest rally in Belgrade against Serbia's president and government
item.description: At least 100,000 people have converged in Belgrade for a massive rally in the Serbian capital seen as a culmination of months-long protests against populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: Jovana Gec And Dusan Stojanovic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why Trump is singling out South Africa and accusing it of being anti-white and anti-American
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The Trump administration’s decision to expel the South African ambassador is its latest move against a country it has singled out for sanctions and accused of being anti-white and anti-American. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope works on signature reform from hospital as Vatican cuts back on updates on medical progress
item.description: Pope Francis has entered the fifth week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia very much looking ahead as he worked on a signature priority of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cuba suffers another massive power outage leaving millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba suffered a massive power outage Friday night affecting Havana and the country's provinces that left millions in the dark. Cuba’s Ministry of Energy and Mines said at around 8:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Netanyahu slams 'terrorist-supporting' UN council that accused Israel of committing sexual crimes
item.description: Israeli and American officials are condemning a report from the United Nations Human Rights Council that accuses Israel of carrying out systemic acts of sexual violence against Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says Hamas sent a toddler to a military outpost
item.description: Israeli soldiers spotted a 4-year-old child approaching their military outpost, who was apparently sent by Hamas. Israel says the boy was returned to Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is stable and showing improvements but will remain hospitalized
item.description: Pope Francis' health has taken a turn for the better this week as he continues to show improvement following a Monday update that listed him as “out of danger from death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Greenland's center-right party pulls off upset victory as Trump seeks control
item.description: Greenland's center-right Demokraatit party saw a sharp increase in support as it won the country's election on Tuesday. Independence became a central issue as President Donald Trump talked of a U.S. takeover.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistani insurgents attack a train carrying hundreds of people and take hostages
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani insurgents attacked a passenger train carrying several hundred people as it passed through a tunnel on Tuesday and claimed to have taken more than 100 hostages, though officials later said at least 104 were rescued.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US resumes military aid and intelligence sharing as Ukraine says it is open to a 30-day ceasefire
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — The Trump administration lifted its suspension of military aid and intelligence sharing for Ukraine, and Kyiv signaled that it was open to a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Russia, pending Moscow’s agreement, American and Ukrainian officials said Tuesday following talks
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Search for Sudiksha Konanki, missing Pitt student, continues in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki was last seen early Thursday morning on a beach near Punta Cana. Police have spoken with a man allegedly with her at the time.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: British tourist detained in ICE center after visa suspension: Family
item.description: In a statement to NewsNation, an ICE spokesperson said Burke was detained by the agency "for violation of the terms and conditions of her admission.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Pope gets good news from doctors: He's no longer in immediate danger
item.description: Doctors are still cautious and have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope thanks volunteers for 'miracle of tenderness' as another Holy Year event passes without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis thanked volunteers Sunday for the “miracle of tenderness” they offer the sick, as he continued his treatment for double pneumonia and doctors reported some positive news.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, NewsNation
